relationshipMesh.relationship: Volcanic heat, magma chambers, crustal fractures, and groundwater circulation create geothermal ecosystems.
Geothermal systems surface as geysers, hot springs, mud pots, fumaroles, and microbial mats.
Thermal water circulation, dissolved minerals, and microbial communities connect Yellowstone to hydrothermal systems.
Hydrothermal systems support thermophiles, extremophiles, microbial mats, and chemosynthetic ecosystems.
Microbial communities transform minerals, sulfur, carbon compounds, and chemical energy.
Heated water transports dissolved minerals, nutrients, and chemical gradients.
Water links volcanic, geothermal, hydrothermal, climate, soil, and biosphere processes.
Volcanic ash and lava weathering produce mineral-rich soils that support ecological succession.
Heat, minerals, water, and chemical gradients make geothermal environments important origin-of-life systems.
